PROCESS TERMINATED

The Workings of the Planck Gate: A Mechanical Analysis of Terminal Ingestion and Phase-Inversion Dynamics

By Barry Curran

READ THE FULL PAPER AT PHILPAPERS:

https://philpapers.org/archive/CURTWO-5.pdf

Welcome to the Singularity

What goes on behind blackness? We've all heard the theories, but never quite like this. Beneath the veil lies a functional recycling mechanism—a mechanical rift in the fabric of the universe.

THE WORKINGS OF THE PLANCK GATE
ABSTRACT: This paper proposes a radical re-engineering of the "Black Hole" phenomenon, defining it not as a mathematical singularity of infinite density, but as a functional recycling mechanism within a larger universal feedback system. By applying the principles of the Geometry of the Fractal Cone, I demonstrate that the "singular point" is actually a Physical Rift in space-time—a Planck Gate designed for terminal resource reclamation. This model defines the Black Hole as a Terminal Resource Reclamation System (TRRS) that ingests high-entropy matter, strips it of local 3D identity, and re-injects it into the internal manifold as expansionary flux-potentiality. In this model, the "engine" of the universe does not break at the singularity; it simply transitions into a high-pressure reclamation phase. I acknowledge established components of black hole physics—including the event horizon, accretion flow, and relativistic jets—not as mysterious anomalies, but as discrete stages of this mechanical ingestion cycle. By aligning these observations with the mechanics of a reciprocal engine, I provide a structural logic for the transition of matter through the horizon, the compression gradients of the interior "Chute," and the ultimate phase-inversion at the Planck Gate. This framework resolves the "Information Paradox" by demonstrating that information is never lost; it is simply shredded, unformatted, and re-poured through the aperture into the foundation of the next universal undulation. — READ THE FULL PAPER AT PHILPAPERS.ORG — URL: HTTPS://PHILPAPERS.ORG/ARCHIVE/CURTWO-5.PDF
